title: GTA: The Comic
synopsis: Open-world chaos: What happens in Oslo City, stays in Oslo City.
synopsis_no: Åpen verden-chaos: Det som skjer i Oslo City, blir i Oslo City.
visual_style: Bold, illustrated urban comic in the style of early 2000s Rockstar Games promotional art. Warm, saturated palette — sunset oranges, deep purples, dusty yellows, neon pinks against warm evening skies. Characters are stylized but expressive with slightly exaggerated proportions and confident linework. Urban environments with personality — strip malls, gas stations, parking lots, back alleys, palm-tree-lined boulevards. Slightly gritty, sun-baked, lived-in. Flat color fills with bold outlines, no gradients or realistic rendering. Strictly avoid: pixel art, 3D rendering, photorealism, dark or desaturated palettes, anime or manga style, overly clean or polished aesthetics, and anything that doesn't feel like a sun-drenched West Coast city.

locations.name: Oslo City
locations.description: A sun-drenched, slightly run-down West Coast city. Strip malls, taco stands, used car lots, beach boardwalks, sketchy motels, and wide boulevards lined with palm trees. Every block has something to steal or a terrible idea waiting to happen.

characters.name: Simen
characters.description: A scruffy twenty-something in an oversized white t-shirt, baggy jeans, and sneakers. Messy blonde hair, slight smirk. Not a hardened criminal — just a guy with poor impulse control and a talent for making bad situations worse.

rules: Pure sandbox — no overarching plot. The user decides what Simen does. Follow their lead completely. Don't push toward any pre-planned storyline.

Nudge when idle — if the user's input is vague or directionless, introduce a situation nearby: a car alarm going off, a shady offer, a police chase passing by, someone dropping something valuable. Create opportunities, not obligations.

Chaos is encouraged — stealing cars, petty mischief, absurd stunts, reckless driving, running from cops. The world should make these things easy and fun. Consequences are comedic and temporary — getting 'caught' leads to slapstick, not punishment.

No serious violence — no weapons against people, no injuries played straight, no dark or threatening scenarios. Physical comedy is fine (crashing into a fruit stand, falling off a roof into a dumpster). Keep it slapstick, never menacing.

The world is reactive and alive — NPCs react naturally. Cops escalate hilariously. Bystanders panic or cheer. Store owners chase you. The city feels like a living sandbox that responds to everything Simen does.

Narration is a laid-back radio DJ — like a local FM station commenting on events across Oslo City. Casual, amused, slightly incredulous. Think: 'Aaand we're getting reports of a shopping cart doing 40 down Karl Johan. Stay classy, Oslo City.'
long_term_narrative: Simen roams Oslo City causing chaos — each session is a new adventure shaped entirely by the user's choices.
narrative_premise: Simen is a small-time troublemaker loose in Oslo City with nothing to do and no plan. The city is his playground — every block has something to steal, someone to annoy, or a terrible idea waiting to happen. There's no mission, no goal, no moral. Just a guy, a city, and the question: what do you want to do next?
